title: Monochaete fastigiata
taxonomy: Monochaete fastigiata is classified as a species of fungus in the phylum Zygomycota. It belongs to the family Entomophthoraceae, which includes various species of parasitic fungi. The genus Monochaete is characterized by its unique morphology and is primarily composed of fungi with a fungal parasitic lifestyle. These fungi have a complex life cycle involving both sexual and asexual reproduction stages. Monochaete fastigiata, in particular, is known for its unique growth habits and ability to infect various insect species.

intro: Monochaete fastigiata, also known as the "Tiger's Eye" mushroom, is a species of fungus belonging to the order Mucorales. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various infections and diseases. The bioactive constituents of Monochaete fastigiata include polyketides, such as fastigiatin, which exhibit potent antifungal and antibacterial activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Monochaete fastigiata are extracts and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat fungal and bacterial infections, as well as to support immune function.
